text.title: okr-setting Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for okr-setting as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Key Results that are tasks: "Publish 12 blog posts", "Launch feature X", "Run 5 user interviews".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tasks measure output, not outcome. Completing the task does not prove the Objective was reached. Rewrite as the outcome the task enables: "Achieve 5,000 monthly organic visitors" instead of "Publish 12 blog posts".

failure-mode.description: Too many OKRs: 7+ Objectives, each with 5 KRs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exceeding the 3–5 Objective cap destroys focus. With 7 Objectives, none gets the sustained attention needed to move metrics. Everything is equally urgent means nothing is actually prioritised.

failure-mode.description: Setting 100% confidence targets to guarantee achievement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sandbagged targets are not OKRs—they are delivery commitments with extra branding. A 100% score every quarter signals that targets need recalibration upward, not celebration.

failure-mode.description: Setting OKRs at the start of the quarter and not reviewing until the end.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Blocked KRs stay blocked without weekly visibility. Weekly check-ins catch blockers at week 3, not week 12.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A KR without a starting point is unmeasurable. "NPS of 50+" without knowing current NPS cannot be scored. Always establish the baseline before setting the target.
